name: DC Offline Response
description: Step-by-step playbook for responding to an offline Domain Controller
author: AD Identity Control Plane

steps.title: Initial Connectivity Check
steps.description: Can you reach the DC via ICMP ping?

steps.title: Check AD Services
steps.description: DC is reachable but may have service issues. Check critical AD services.

steps.title: Check Physical/VM Status
steps.description: DC is not responding to network. Is this a physical server or virtual machine?

steps.title: VM Console Access
steps.description: Access the VM console through your hypervisor (Hyper-V Manager, vSphere, etc.). Check if the VM is powered on, check for crash dumps or blue screens. If powered off, check the event log on the host for reasons.

steps.title: Physical Access Required
steps.description: Contact the data center or on-site team to check: Power status (LEDs, PSU), Console output (crash screen, POST errors), Network cable connectivity, iLO/iDRAC/IPMI remote management.

steps.title: Check NTDS Database Integrity
steps.description: Before restarting NTDS, verify the database is not corrupt.

steps.title: Restart DNS Service
steps.description: DNS is critical for AD functionality. Verify DNS zones are loaded and check for event log errors related to DNS. If DNS depends on AD-integrated zones, NTDS must be running first.

steps.title: Multiple Service Failure - Consider Reboot
steps.description: Multiple critical services are stopped. This often indicates a more serious issue. Check Event Viewer for the root cause. A controlled reboot may be the fastest resolution, but ensure replication is current first.

steps.title: Check LDAP and Kerberos Connectivity
steps.description: Services are running but DC appears offline. Test LDAP and Kerberos ports.

steps.title: DC Recovery Status
steps.description: Were you able to bring the DC back online?

steps.title: Verify DC Health
steps.description: Run a full health check to verify the DC is back to healthy state.

steps.title: Escalation Required
steps.description: DC cannot be recovered. Actions needed: 1) Verify FSMO roles are not held by this DC (transfer if needed), 2) Check if this DC is the only GC in its site, 3) Notify infrastructure team, 4) Plan DC metadata cleanup if permanent failure, 5) Update DNS records.

steps.title: Verify Replication
steps.description: Ensure replication is healthy after recovery.

steps.title: Document the Incident
steps.description: Record the following: 1) When the issue was detected, 2) Root cause (if identified), 3) Actions taken, 4) Resolution time, 5) Follow-up items (hardware replacement, capacity planning, etc.)
